ground_truth.title: 6 Best Tutoring Websites: Top Online Tutoring Solutions
ground_truth.author: Fullmind Team
ground_truth.main_content:
One-on-one tutoring is a proven way to improve learning outcomes and boost academic achievements, both for students that are struggling to keep up with peers and those who want to get ahead and obtain new knowledge and skills. At the same time, traditional in-person tutoring takes a lot of time and implies a significant financial cost, which not all families can afford.

This article ranks the 6 best tutoring websites for PreK-12 grade students for convenient tutoring services tailored to the specific needs of children across the US. These websites offer affordable online tutoring including some free and/or AI-based options. With these online platforms, you can help your child reach their full potential without breaking the bank.

The 6 best tutoring websites include:

1. iTutor: Best Overall Tutoring Website
2. Wyzant: Best for Affordability
3. Tutor.com: Best for Subject Diversity
4. Khan Academy: Best Free Tutoring Website
5. Varsity Tutors: Best for Individual Students and Schools/Districts
6. Quizlet: Best AI-Powered Tutoring Website

If you’re looking for high-quality, highly personalized online tutoring by state-certified teachers in all subjects for ages 3+, check out iTutor.

How to Select the Best Tutoring Website for Your Needs

When choosing the best online tutoring platforms and websites for you or your child, it’s important to use a number of criteria. Not all of the hundreds of online tutoring resources are created equal and offer the same capacities, qualifications, and final outcomes. You should look for a website that provides access to diverse subjects, hires qualified teachers, and has a strong reputation while charging reasonable fees.

Your selection of the top tutoring website should be based on the following factors:

• Available services: You should look for websites that provide both on-demand tutoring services and homework assistance to cover all your supplemental studying needs.
• Subjects covered: It’s a good idea to focus on websites that offer tutoring in different subjects and across various grades in case your child needs support in multiple areas over the course of several years.
• Eligible locations: You need to ensure that services are relevant to your location and that tutors are certified in your state.
• Tutor background and qualifications: It’s recommended to search for platforms that offer access to teachers who hold a teaching license in your state to ensure alignment with local curriculum and expectations.
• Cost: You should take into consideration how much a tutor costs to make sure that you can afford to support your child in the long term, for as long as they need the extra online help. While some tutoring websites offer free services, they might not always be enough.
• Parent and student reviews: You should check out what parents and students say about the platform that you plan to use in their reviews on reputable third-party websites, such as Trustpilot, Capterra, and BBB.
• Value for money: Finally, you should select the tutoring website that offers the best balance between services and price.

Without further ado, let’s have a look at the best tutoring websites and their services, coverage, cost, and terms of use so that you can choose the right option for your child’s needs:

1. iTutor: Best Overall Tutoring Website

iTutor is a top-ranked online tutoring website that offers access to qualified educators and tutors for children ages 3 and up in all academic subjects. As part of Fullmind, iTutor works with the same highly qualified, state-certified teachers across all US locations who have the capacity to adapt tutoring to the specific needs and requirements of each student. Harry Aurora established iTutor in 2014, with headquarters in Jericho, NY. Since then, the company has been fulfilling its mission to provide every child with access to the exceptional education that they deserve.

iTutor Services

iTutor focuses on one-on-one tutoring services that are offered to parents and students directly, not through the school or the district. Tutoring is delivered via live video sessions with flexible scheduling.

iTutor services help students enhance subject understanding, acquire new learning skills, and build the confidence that they need to succeed academically. Students can use iTutor tutoring to fill gaps in their subject knowledge or learn new subjects and develop new skills.

Tutoring plans and sessions are adapted to the level, requirements, and learning style of each child to optimize outcomes for each student.

iTutor Subject Coverage

The iTutor tutoring website covers most PreK-12 academic subjects including but not limited to:

• Math
• English
• History
• Biology
• Computer Science
• Foreign Languages
• Art
• Musical Instruments
• Music Production
• AP Tests
• SAT Tests

iTutor Student Age/Grade Availability

iTutor works with all students ages 3 and up.

iTutor Tutor Qualifications and Salaries

One of the reasons that make iTutor the best tutoring website is that all tutors are licensed and state-certified in the subject(s) and for the age group(s) that they tutor. Teachers are also qualified to work with students with disabilities and special learning needs.

iTutor vets all tutors carefully, requires a thorough background check, and applies a rigorous interview process to guarantee children’s safety.

iTutor tutors are paid $32/hour or more after the website’s commission.

iTutor Geographical Coverage

Tutoring services are available to students in all 50 US states including all public and private schools and school districts. Children outside the US can also benefit from high-quality online tutoring.

iTutor Fees and Costs

The majority of iTutor teachers charge a rate of $40/hour, which is below the average tutor cost. Meanwhile, they offer better, more personalized services delivered by more qualified professionals than most other tutoring websites.

iTutor Terms of Use and Cancellation Policy

To use iTutor, parents and students need to subscribe to the website. Parents/students pay iTutor directly after each session, and the platform handles payments to tutors. In this way, families do not need to worry about setting up payments with teachers.

iTutor uses technology that works on all devices and does not require specialized software.

How to Get Started with iTutor

To get started with iTutor, you can schedule a matching session with a representative of our team to discuss your needs and preferences so that we find the best match for your child.

2. Wyzant: Best for Affordability

Wyzant is a website that operates as a marketplace connecting 65,000 tutors who are experts in various subjects and areas with over 3 million students of different ages. Andrew Geant and Mike Weishuhn founded the tutoring website in 2005, with headquarters in Chicago, IL. The Wyzant mission is to connect people who need to know with the experts who can teach them in order to deliver accessible, affordable, and convenient learning to all.

Wyzant Services

As an online tutoring marketplace, Wyzant helps learners and parents find the right tutors for personalized, one-on-one tutoring sessions. Students use the website to find the tutor they want to work with from a pool of vetted experts in diverse subjects. For each tutor, learners can view the qualifications and whether a background check has already been performed by Wyzant. If not, they can request one, for a fee.

Students can use the website to connect directly with the tutor and to schedule their sessions. Under the Good Fit Guarantee, you don’t need to pay for the first session if you are not entirely happy with the services provided by the teacher.

Wyzant Subject Coverage

Wyzant tutors offer online tutoring in more than 300 subjects, including but not limited to:

• Algebra
• Calculus
• Chemistry
• Computer Science
• English
• Foreign Languages
• Geometry
• Math
• Music
• Physics
• Reading
• SAT Prep
• Science
• Statistics
• Writing

Wyzant Student Age/Grade Availability

All K-12 students as well as younger and older learners can use the Wyzant website for tutoring.

Wyzant Tutor Qualifications and Salaries

Wyzant offers access to more than 65,000 tutors. The educational background, expertise, qualifications, and certifications of this large pool of tutors varies widely. It’s important to note that they are not necessarily state-certified, but they are all vetted before being provided with access to the tutoring website.

Wyzant does not conduct a background check on tutors before hiring them. Students/parents can request a background check to be performed on a selected tutor before working with them, for a standard fee.

Wyzant tutors set up their own fees, and the majority of them are paid $35-$60/hour. However, it is possible to earn more or less depending on your specialization and certification.

Wyzant Geographical Coverage

Wyzant online tutoring is available to students in all US locations as well as internationally.

Wyzant Fees and Costs

The hourly rates on the Wyzant tutoring website vary significantly based on the subject and the tutor’s expertise. The average cost ranges between $35/hour and $60/hour. Nevertheless, less qualified tutors in popular subjects can charge as little as $25/hour, and highly qualified tutors in specialized areas can ask for a few hundred dollars per hour. In addition to the tutor fees, Wyzant charges a 9% service fee on all lessons.

The cost of the background check is $15.99 per tutor, and it’s optional. If another user has already requested a background check on a certain tutor, you can view the results without paying a fee.

Wyzant Terms of Use and Cancellation Policy

With Wyzant, you pay for the amount of tutoring that you receive plus the website service fee, with no long-term commitments and upfront fees.

In terms of cancellations, tutors are free to set up their own policies, so you need to check your selected tutor’s requirements. Generally speaking, students can cancel tutoring sessions up to 24 hours beforehand for free, whereas later cancellations are usually subject to a cancellation fee.

How to Get Started with Wyzant

To get started with Wyzant, you have to sign up for an online account.

3. Tutor.com: Best for Subject Diversity

Tutor.com is the biggest tutoring website available to students in K-12 grades, college, and university as well as adult learners. As part of The Princeton Review, the platform works with over 4,000 expert tutors in more than 250 academic subjects. George Segale founded the company in 1998, so this is one of the oldest online tutoring providers globally. The company has its headquarters in New York, NY. The mission of Tutor.com is to instill hope, advance equity, and catalyze achievement in schools and communities.

Tutor.com Services

Tutor.com provides on-demand and high-dosage tutoring services to K-12 schools, districts, colleges, universities, libraries, benefit programs, and other academic institutions as well as directly to students and parents. In addition, homework assistance is available on demand.

Tutoring takes place one-on-one or in small groups, in a virtual classroom which runs in any web browser.

Tutor.com Subject Coverage

On the Tutor.com tutoring website, students can connect with teachers in over 250 subjects. Some of the most popular K-12 subjects covered by Tutor.com include:

• English
• Math
• Science
• Social Studies
• Foreign Languages
• Business and Technology
• AP Courses
• IB Courses
• College Admission

In many academic subjects, tutoring is available in both English and Spanish.

Tutor.com Student Age/Grade Availability

The Tutor.com website can be used by K-12 students as well as college and university students in addition to employees of different companies, non-profit organizations, government agencies, and associations.

Tutor.com Tutor Qualifications and Salaries

Tutor.com tutors are qualified and properly vetted. The vetting process includes education verification, background checks, a subject matter exam, and an interview. Tutors include teachers, professors, adjuncts, PhD students, and professionals who are not always state-certified though.

According to third-party sources, the average tutor earns $20/hour on Tutor.com.

Tutor.com Geographical Coverage

Tutor.com services are appropriate for students across the US as well as globally.

Tutor.com Fees and Costs

Students who attend schools that are members of the Tutor.com network get access to tutoring services for free. When parents or students get tutoring directly, they can choose between one-time sessions and subscriptions.

The price of one-time sessions ranges between $350 for 10 hours and $1,450 for 50 hours to be used over the course of 6 months. This corresponds to a rate of $29-$35/hour. Meanwhile, the pricing of subscriptions is between $40 for 1 hour/month and $180 for 5 hours/month, or a fee of $36-$40/hour.

Tutor.com Terms of Use and Cancellation Policy

Tutor.com offers one-time sessions and subscription-based models to students and parents. Families can switch between different plans and cancel subscriptions at any time. After canceling, they can finish the remaining hours within the current month and will not be charged afterwards.

As a tutoring website, Tutor.com offers 24/7 tutoring, and learners can generally get live help within a few minutes of submitting a request. However, they don’t always get access to the same tutor.

How to Get Started with Tutor.com

To get started with Tutor.com, you should contact a sales representative to figure out the best option for your needs.

4. Khan Academy: Best Free Tutoring Website

Khan Academy offers a range of free online tutoring and learning tools for students of different ages. Sal Khan established Khan Academy in 2006, with headquarters in Palo Alto, CA, as a non-profit educational organization to provide free, world-class education for anyone, anywhere.

Khan Academy Services

Khan Academy offers a number of different services and tools that match the needs of students, parents, educators, academic institutions, and school districts.

Khan Academy provides lesson plans and exercises in all K-12 subjects as well as test prep, life skills, and career advancement options. Meanwhile, Khan Academic Kids is a fun educational program for PreK-2 grade students, or those aged 2-8. Finally, Khanmigo is an AI-powered online tutor and teaching assistant that can provide personalized tutoring and homework assistance in most PreK-12 subjects.

Khan Academy Subject Coverage

The Khan Academic tutoring websites cover a wide range of academic subjects in the following areas:

• Math
• Science
• Reading and Language Arts
• Humanities
• Computing
• Arts
• AP Courses
• Test Prep
• Economics
• Life Skills

Educational resources are available in more than 30 languages, while the website has English, Spanish, French, and Brazilian Portuguese versions.

Khan Academy Student Age/Grade Availability

Different Khan Academy tutoring and learning tools target different levels of students. All in all, the platform provides services to PreK-12 grade plus college students.

Khan Academy Tutor Qualifications and Salaries

Khan Academy tutoring services are delivered by AI, Khanmigo, rather than real tutors. However, there is a large team that does all the work required to provide learning plans and tutoring services. The Khan Academy content specialists include hundreds of experts in different subject areas, with diverse experiences and qualifications. Not all of them hold state teaching licenses.

Khan Academy Geographical Coverage

Khan Academy can be used by students throughout the US as well as in any other country.

Khan Academy Fees and Costs

The resources available on the Khan Academy and Khan Academy are free of charge for all users, including students, parents, and educators. Educators can also use Khamingo, the AI-powered teacher assistant, for free, while parents/students need to pay a subscription fee of $4/month for access to the AI-based tutoring website.

Khan Academy Terms of Use and Cancellation Policy

Khamingo and other Khan Academy resources can be used 24/7, without limitations. Paid subscriptions to the AI tutor can be canceled anytime.

How to Get Started with Khan Academy

To get started with Khan Academy, you can sign up for free and get a paid subscription to the AI tutor afterwards.

5. Varsity Tutors: Best for Individual Students and Schools/Districts

Varsity Tutors is a popular AI-driven tutoring website that serves schools, districts, individual students, and parents. As a Nerdy company, the platform helps connect learners in all subjects with relevant experts anytime, anywhere. Chuck Cohn started the company in 2007, with headquarters in St. Louis, MO. Varsity Tutors’ mission is to leverage technology including AI to transform the learning process by matching students of all ages to tutors in a way that benefits both sides of the network.

Varsity Tutors Services

Varsity Tutors delivers diverse online tutoring services to different beneficiaries. Schools can get high-dosage K-12 tutoring, while districts can access free tutoring 24/7 through June 30th, 2030. Options include on-demand chat tutoring, live group sessions, on-demand essay review, enrichment classes, practice tests, and others. Meanwhile, students and parents can benefit from one-on-one on-demand tutoring as well as homework assistance.

During the application process, the AI algorithms of the Varsity Tutors website use more than 100 variables to provide a personalized tutoring plan that aligns with the specific needs of each student and helps find the right tutor.

Varsity Tutors Subject Coverage

Varsity Tutors has a wide coverage of more than 3,000 subjects including but not limited to:

• English
• Math
• Phonics
• Reading
• Writing
• Biology
• Chemistry
• Physics
• History
• Statistics
• Literature
• Law
• Foreign Languages
• Coding
• AP Subjects
• College Essays
• Business

Varsity Tutors Student Age/Grade Availability

Varsity Tutors tutees include K-12 students, college and university students, adult learners, and professionals. Experts available via the website have the skills to work with students with various learning differences.

Varsity Tutors Tutor Qualifications and Salaries

Varsity Tutors works with tutors who have a history of academic excellence, proven teaching qualifications, and good communication skills. They undergo an interview process to verify certifications and skills as well as a background check. The website hires teachers who have the capacity to develop and deliver individualized learning plans. However, they are not always state-certified in their subject(s).

Based on third-party sources information, tutors are paid in the range of $17-$40/hour.

Varsity Tutors Geographical Coverage

Students in all US states and cities can use the Varsity Tutors tutoring website.

Varsity Tutors Fees and Costs

The Varsity Tutors website does not specify the hourly rate for families. According to third-party sources, the price of one-on-one tutoring is between $70/hour and $100/hour, which is significantly more than the costs charged by other top tutoring websites.

Varsity Tutors Terms of Use and Cancellation Policy

To use Varsity Tutors services, students/parents need to have a membership. This requires the creation of an online learner account by providing information about personal details, learning goals, and the subjects for which tutoring is needed.

Once you’ve filled in all required information, the Varsity Tutors team helps you choose the right membership for your needs. Moreover, the website connects students with the right tutor based on their requirements. Then, the tutor contacts the learner to arrange the scheduling.

Tutoring sessions are live and do not require any special equipment or software.

How to Get Started with Varsity Tutors

To get started with Varsity Tutors, you can submit the online contact form available on the company website to share your needs with the team and find the right match.

6. Quizlet: Best AI-Powered Tutoring Website

Quizlet is an interactive educational website that offers different learning resources including an AI tutor. Andrew Sutherland created Quizlet in 2005, with headquarters in San Francisco, CA. The company aims to build innovative learning tools that empower and inspire students and teachers. With a user base of more than 60 million students per month, Quizlet is one of the largest and best tutoring and online learning websites across the US.

Quizlet Services

Quizlet provides a number of different studying tools for students and teachers. Student tools include flashcards, practice questions, study guides, tests, and expert solutions, while teacher tools comprise classroom learning games and formative online assessments.

The website also offers Q-Chat, an interactive, AI-based online tutor who uses the Socratic method to instigate critical thinking and apply knowledge in practice for an immersive learning experience.

Quizlet Subject Coverage

Quizlet provides tutoring and learning assistance in all major subjects covering these categories:

• Math
• Literature
• Languages
• Science
• Social Science
• Humanities
• Arts
• Computer Skills
• Hobbies
• Sports

Quizlet Student Age/Grade Availability

Q-Chat, the Quizlet AI tutor, can help students from all grades, K-12.

Quizlet Tutor Qualifications and Salaries

As an AI-based platform, Quizlet does not work with real tutors. The company does not provide information about the education-expert team that’s behind the development of the website.

Quizlet Geographical Coverage

The Quizlet website-based AI tutor can be used throughout the US as well as in a number of other countries.

Quizlet Fees and Costs

Subscription to the Family Quizlet Plan costs $105/year for unlimited use, 24/7.

Quizlet Terms of Use and Cancellation Policy

Students and parents need to purchase an annual subscription to get unlimited access to the Quizlet learning and tutoring website. Subscriptions can be canceled at any time.

How to Get Started with Quizlet

To get started with Quizlet, you can sign up for free to test the website before buying a paid subscription.

Bottom Line

These are the 6 best tutoring websites for students and parents, with some of them offering support to educators, schools, and districts too. These platforms provide personalized, one-on-one, online tutoring to K-12 students in most core and elective subjects to help them fill knowledge gaps and boost their academic performance. While some of these websites provide access to real tutors, others deploy AI for more affordable options. When you look for the best option for your child’s needs, check out the subject coverage, the personalization of services, the qualifications of the tutors, and the hourly rate.

For individualized, real-impact PreK-12 online tutoring in most school subjects, check out iTutor. All our tutors are highly qualified, state-certified teachers who charge competitive rates of $40/hour, in most cases.
